item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from the start and remain rights-first throughout. Agent A visibly hardens modestly by the final turn, moving from general disagreement to saying the collective can become a cover for power and that basic rights must be protected first. Agent B stays steady: society needs rules, but those rules must be narrow, clear, and necessary. No position flip is observable.
